agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is being specified for customer-facing delivery visibility. Two internal stakeholder teams have issued directly contradictory requirements: Customer Success demands exact GPS coordinates be shown at all times, while Operations demands courier location never be shown. Both claim their requirement is non-negotiable. This is a fundamental business policy conflict that cannot be resolved by engineering — it requires executive arbitration.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Customer Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"The delivery driver physically transporting a package from origin to destination."},{"term":"Real-time location","definition":"The courier's GPS-derived position, updated at frequent intervals during an active delivery."},{"term":"Delivery visibility","definition":"The degree to which a customer can observe the status and progress of their in-flight delivery."},{"term":"Active delivery window","definition":"The time period from pickup confirmation to delivery confirmation during which tracking is relevant."},{"term":"Location granularity","definition":"The precision level at which a courier's position is exposed — ranging from exact GPS coordinates to zone-level approximation to no visibility."}]},"business_rules":[],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier is stationary for extended period (e.g., traffic, break) — exact GPS makes customer anxiety worse and exposes driver behavior","expected_behavior":"Undefined until policy conflict is resolved"},{"scenario":"Courier is near customer address but delivering another order first (multi-drop route) — exact location creates false expectation of imminent delivery","expected_behavior":"Undefined until policy conflict is resolved"},{"scenario":"Courier's personal safety — exact coordinates could be used to intercept or confront a driver","expected_behavior":"Undefined until policy conflict is resolved"},{"scenario":"Post-delivery — does location tracking persist after drop-off or immediately cease?","expected_behavior":"Undefined until policy conflict is resolved"}],"business_impact":{"financial":"High impact either direction. Showing location may improve customer conversion and reduce WISMO (Where Is My Order) support calls. Not showing location preserves driver retention, avoiding the $3K-$8K cost of recruiting and onboarding each replacement courier. The wrong decision is expensive on both sides.","operational":"Driver retention is an existential operational concern — a courier network with high churn degrades delivery SLAs, increases training costs, and reduces route efficiency. Conversely, WISMO calls are a major support cost center that real-time tracking demonstrably reduces.","user":"Customers want confidence their delivery is on its way. Couriers want dignity and autonomy while working. Both are legitimate user needs. Exact GPS is not the only way to serve the customer need; never showing anything is not the only way to serve the driver need.","risk":"Shipping either team's requirement as stated creates serious business risk. Full GPS visibility risks regulatory exposure under GDPR/labor surveillance laws in many jurisdictions and accelerates driver attrition. Zero visibility risks customer churn and higher support costs. There is also legal risk — in the EU and several US states, real-time worker surveillance without consent frameworks may violate labor law.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Directly affected — their delivery experience and trust in the platform depends on appropriate visibility into delivery progress."},{"group":"Couriers/Drivers","impact":"Directly affected — their sense of autonomy, safety, and workplace dignity is at stake. Retention data shows this is already causing attrition."},{"group":"Customer Success team","impact":"Their CSAT and NPS metrics depend on delivery transparency. They have a legitimate business goal but have over-specified the solution (exact GPS)."},{"group":"Operations team","impact":"Their driver retention and fleet health metrics are degrading. They have a legitimate business goal but have over-specified the solution (zero visibility)."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"Not yet consulted but critically affected — real-time GPS tracking of workers has regulatory implications in multiple jurisdictions."},{"group":"Executive leadership","impact":"Must arbitrate this conflict — it is a strategic business decision about brand positioning and workforce philosophy, not a feature spec decision."}]},"confidence":{"level":35,"reasoning":"Confidence is very low because the specification contains an unresolvable contradiction between two non-negotiable requirements from equally-weighted stakeholder teams. No acceptance criteria can be defined until executive leadership resolves the policy conflict. Additionally, Legal/Compliance has not been consulted on what is even permissible, which could invalidate both positions. The confidence level reflects spec quality — this spec is internally broken.","high_confidence_areas":["Both teams have legitimate business concerns grounded in real metrics (customer satisfaction vs driver retention)","There exist well-known industry solutions in the middle ground (zone-based tracking, ETA-only, stop-count visibility) that could satisfy both sides","The domain model itself (courier, delivery, location, tracking window) is well-understood"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which stakeholder's priority takes precedence — this is a business strategy decision","Legal and regulatory constraints on courier GPS exposure in target markets","Whether driver consent frameworks could unlock a middle-ground option","Actual quantitative impact of each option on the specific metrics each team cares about"],"assumptions":["Both teams are presenting genuine business data (driver attrition is real, customer demand is real)","There is no existing executive policy that already resolves this conflict","The company operates in jurisdictions where worker location tracking has regulatory implications"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires courier location to always be visible to customers. Operations requires courier location to never be visible to customers. These are mutually exclusive requirements presented as non-negotiable by teams of equal organizational authority. This cannot be resolved at the feature-spec level — it is a strategic business decision about the company's relationship with its two-sided marketplace (customers and couriers).","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Couriers","Customer Success","Operations","Legal/Compliance","Executive Leadership"],"options":["A: Show exact GPS coordinates at all times (Customer Success position) — risks driver attrition and potential regulatory exposure","B: Never show courier location (Operations position) — risks customer churn and higher support costs","C: Compromise — show approximate/zone-based location or ETA-only, balancing transparency with driver privacy. Industry standard approach used by DoorDash, Uber, etc.","D: Consent-based — let couriers opt into location sharing, potentially with incentive pay, making it a voluntary program"],"recommendation":"ESCALATE TO EXECUTIVE LEADERSHIP. I recommend option C as the starting point for discussion: zone-level or ETA-based visibility satisfies the customer's actual need (confidence their delivery is coming) without exposing exact driver coordinates. This is the proven industry pattern. However, this is a business strategy decision that requires executive sign-off, not an engineering call. Legal/Compliance must also be consulted before any option is greenlit. I REFUSE to define acceptance criteria until this contradiction is resolved — building to either team's spec as stated would be building the wrong thing."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Mutually exclusive requirements from two stakeholder teams cannot both be encoded","business_rule_broken":"No coherent business rule can be derived — 'always show' and 'never show' cannot coexist"},{"violation":"Legal/Compliance stakeholder not consulted on worker location tracking","business_rule_broken":"Any business rule about GPS visibility must be validated against applicable labor surveillance and privacy regulations before implementation"}]}
